Amber Grace Hulsey  
October 2, 2024  
Tesla, TSLA, Beta: 1.544599894, Current Stock Price: 248.70  
Tesla is a company that makes electric cars and clean energy products. Its business segments are automotive and energy generation and storage. Tesla's products run on electricity and have advanced self-driving features. It also sells solar panels and batteries to strive for renewable energy instead of fossil fuels.  
Some of the industry's main drivers include innovation, strong brand, and leadership. Because of Elon Musk's amazing leadership strategies, Tesla is constantly updating its products to meet the most modern standards possible. Their biggest competitors are Chinese car companies (BYD, for example), Ford Motor Company, and Voltswagen (*What Are Tesla's (TSLA) Main Competitors?*, 2024).   
I first collected daily data from *Yahoo Finance* from 2019 to 2024 to compute beta. This includes recent data and the number of observations, which gives an accurate beta estimate. Going too far back in time does not provide a precise explanation because trends change every second, so the more recent, the better. After gathering the company's stock data for computing beta, I also gathered the S\&P 500 stock data because this index allows for the comparison between the company and, roughly, the overall market. There are many different markets, but the S\&P 500 measures the 500 largest companies at the time, Tesla being in this number.   
	The next step is to compute the daily returns for both Tesla and the market (S\&P 500\) using the Holding Period Return formula. I used four different methods to calculate and confirm beta: Slope, Beta, Regression, and a Scatterplot. For the slope method, I utilized Excel and found the slope between the Holding Period Return of Tesla and the S\&P 500\. To find the beta method, It is the covariance of Tesla and the market divided by the market variance. During method three, Regression looks at the relationship between Tesla and the S\&P 500 to calculate beta. Lastly, beta can be found through the trendline equation using the Scatterplot method. Since all four methods produced the same value, I predict the beta to be 1.544599894 for the Tesla company. 

**![][image1]**  
	Above is a picture of the scatterplot method, which uses the trendline to find beta. The X-Values is the HPR for the Market Index (S\&P 500), and the Y-Values is the HPR for Tesla.   
		Beta values can range between 0 and 2\. The market beta is 1, and we can use this benchmark to compare the company to the market. Tesla has a beta of 1.544599894, which is an excellent value compared to the market. However, Tesla is 54.5% more volatile than the market. This means Tesla has a higher risk than the market and a greater rate of return. Being more volatile than the market means it rises or falls more rapidly.   
	When explaining this beta value to an investor, it is best to provide both sides. Since Tesla is more volatile than the market, there is a greater risk of losing money. However, a higher return rate is also if the stock performs well. Investors willing to take the risk have a greater chance of receiving a return rate than the S\&P 500\. Traders prefer higher betas because they will only take on the risk quickly. For example, if a trader buys a Tesla stock but begins losing money, they only lose money for a short time. However, a long-term investor may not want a stock if the beta is relatively high because if it begins losing money, it could plummet quickly.   
	Automotive companies have higher betas because they are, on average, more volatile than the market (Infront Analytics, 2024). Cars have different types of technology, and customers pay a higher price relative to earnings, which creates more risk. Since the number of electrical car companies is growing, Tesla is gaining more competitors, creating more risk for investors wanting to invest in a stock. Any company involving tons of technology produces risky stocks because one business decision can make or break a company. Since Tesla utilizes technology in all its products, it is no surprise that its beta is above the market.   
	As of October 2, 2024, at 10:30 a.m., Tesla's current stock price is $248.70. Even though this is a specific price, company stock prices change every second. A company's stock price represents the present value of the stock to both buyers and sellers through the cost of one share of the stock. For Tesla, purchasing one stock would cost $248.70.   
	In conclusion, Tesla is more volatile than the S\&P 500, which has costs and benefits. Being more risky than the market means that when the market is doing well, so are the returns for the stocks. However, it also means that when the market is doing poorly, so are the returns for the stocks. For someone who does not mind investing in a risky stock, Tesla is the perfect choice. 

References

Infront Analytics. “Group 1 Automotive Inc.: Levered/Unlevered Beta (GPI | USA | Specialty Retailers) \- Infront Analytics.” *Infrontanalytics.com*, 2024, www.infrontanalytics.com/fe-EN/34449NU/Group-1-Automotive-Inc-/Beta. Accessed 1 Oct. 2024\.

Reason, L. (2021, February 4). *How Has Tesla Achieved Its Leading Position in the Automotive Industry? \- Guidepoint*. Guidepoint. https://www.guidepoint.com/advisor-blog-ja-jp/how-has-tesla-achieved-its-leading-position-in-the-automotive-industry/

*What Are Tesla’s (TSLA) Main Competitors?* (2024). Investopedia. https://www.investopedia.com/ask/answers/120314/who-are-teslas-tsla-main-competitors.asp

*What Makes Tesla’s Business Model Different?* (2024). Investopedia. https://www.investopedia.com/articles/active-trading/072115/what-makes-teslas-business-model-different.asp  
*Yahoo Finance*. (2024). Yahoo.com. https://finance.yahoo.com/
